feed.title: blank
feed.subtitle: Computational biologist — palaeogenomics, ancient DNA, and population genetics. Lead Computational Biologist at GenomeVault and Postdoctoral Researcher at the Centre for Palaeogenetics, Stockholm University.

entry.title: Welcome to my blog
entry.content:
Welcome, and thanks for stopping by. This is the first post on my new site.

I plan to use this space to write, from time to time, about the things I work on day to day: bioinformatics for ancient and degraded DNA, reproducible pipelines (mostly Nextflow), read-mapping and variant-calling strategies for damaged sequences, target-capture design, and the occasional note on tools and workflows I find useful.

Posts will be a mix of short technical notes, tutorials, and reflections on doing computational research. To write a new post, add a Markdown file to the _posts/ folder named YYYY-MM-DD-title.md — this file is a working example you can copy.

More soon.
entry.summary: A short introduction and what I plan to write about here.
